About

Artist, Lecturer,
Curator

Lavoslava Benčić is a Slovenian intermedia artist, photographer and multimedia lecturer based in Ljubljana. Her practice spans interactive electronic installations, e-textiles, AI art, glitch art, sound art, photography and video art.

She has been photographing since 1982 and has exhibited internationally in Slovenia, Croatia, Italy, the UK, USA, Austria, Romania, Serbia, Turkey, Denmark and beyond.

She is a habilitated higher education lecturer in multimedia production (2017) and teaches at the Institute and Academy of Multimedia in Ljubljana and as visiting lecturer at the UNG Academy of Arts in Nova Gorica.

She also works as curator and editor — notably at Photo Art Gallery Batana in Rovinj, Croatia.

Works

Selected Works

Electronics / Interactive

Electronic Labyrinths

Interactive electronic installation inviting viewers into a labyrinth of sound and light.

Electronics / Interactive

The Concert of Plants

Audible interactive poetic plants — installation that converts electrical signals of plants into sound.

Digital / AI Art

Pollination

A collection of seven AI-generated works — a homage to botanical illustrators whose drawings trained deep learning models.

Digital / Laser Cutting

Trunchet Tiles

Laser-cut acrylic multiples exploring generative tiling patterns derived from Sebastien Truchet's 1704 treatise.

Sound / Video

Industrial Silence Kitchen

Animated GIFs as silent representations of industrial soundscapes — vectorscopes, spectrograms and waveforms without sound.

🎵
Sound / E-Textiles

E-textile Loudspeakers

Loudspeakers integrated into textile — an experiment at the boundary of fashion and sound art.

🌊
Sound / Urban

Restless Blue Spreads Through the Town

Audio-visual project about urban restlessness and colour in the city.

🤖
Sound / AI

MuseGAN2

Generative music with artificial intelligence — an experiment with neural networks and composition.

🧤
Wearable / Interactive

Poetic Interactive Glove

A glove with embedded sensors that generates poetic text and sound through hand gestures.

🎸
Wearable / Sound

Body Symphony — Quantum body hack

Wearable technology that transforms body movement into a musical composition.

🌿
Wearable / Nature

Nature_I Wear Electrified

Wearable installation at the boundary of nature and electronics — electrified garments inspired by nature.

💊
Wearable / Health

Audible Healing Pressure Points Glove

A glove that sonically interprets acupressure points — a fusion of medicine and art.

🗺️
Digital / Virtual

FETI map

Interactive digital map — awarded project at Imperial College London, 2015.

📦
Digital / Education

Digital Story Cubes

Digital storytelling tool — project recognised at FutureEverything Festival, Manchester 2015.

💀
Digital / Video

La Danza de la Muerte

Video art project interpreting the theme of memento mori through digital media.

📐
Digital / 3D

3D Timespace

Three-dimensional visualisation of time and space — an experiment with digital sculpture.

Digital / AI Art

Pollination

2022 AI generated latent video Lavoslava Benčić

The collection of seven generated graphic works entitled Pollination is a homage to seven botanical illustrators who have accurately documented numerous plant species and thus created basic structures to train deep learning models from text to image in their style.

Author Lavoslava Benčić
Year 2022
Technology AI generated latent video
Public Presentations Modra črta: Od renesanse do novih medijev, Majski salon ZDSLU, Maribor (Slovenia), 2022

Digital / Laser Cutting

Trunchet Tiles

2021 Laser cutting multiples Lavoslava Benčić

Truchet tiles originate from Sebastien Truchet's treatise published in 1704, Mémoir sur les Combinaisons, in which Truchet explores the types of patterns that can be made with a simple square tile divided diagonally, white on one side and black on the other. Cyril Stanley Smith popularized this idea in his 1987 paper, Sebastian Truchet's Tiling Patterns and the Topology of Structural Hierarchy. When Truchet tiles are used in random orientations, the curves combine to form connected lines or faces, creating an infinite number of patterns.

Author Lavoslava Benčić
Year 2021
Technology Laser cutting multiples
Public Presentations Poti novega in multipli, Galerija DLUL, Ljubljana (Slovenia), 2021
Sound / Video

Industrial Silence Kitchen

2020 Video / Audio Lavoslava Benčić

The starting point for creating the collection of GIFs are recordings and reproductions of typical sound patterns in industrial environments. Animated GIFs are integrated representations of sounds (soundwaves, spectrogram, vectorscope) — but without sound. The absence of sound in GIF animations is an extremely appropriate comparison with the industry that has been silent in the Labin area, and its influence is still visible and ubiquitous in the landscape, architecture and people there.

Author Lavoslava Benčić
Year 2020
Technology Video / Audio
Public Presentations IndustriAll, Udruga Metamedij, Labin (Croatia), 2020
Dune Jump ↗ La Lutte ↗ Wa-Wa ↗ Wusch-Usch ↗
Electronics / Interactive

Electronic Labyrinths

2022 Arduino AI generated image Laser cutting Audio synthesis Lavoslava Benčić

Electronic Labyrinths are an interactive modular installation. This project deals with the topic of finding ways out for our civilization, which is plagued by many natural and social problems. My goal is to create working mazes that respond to a random user's presence in the nature.

This project covers the use of several techniques: generating motifs/visual content by machine learning models (text to image), materializing multilayered labyrinths (laser cutter), placing and connecting electronic devices (modules) in / on mazes and finally distribute them in the nature.

Author

Lavoslava Benčić

Year

2022

Technology

Audio synthesis, AI generated image, Arduino, laser cutting

Public Presentations

Transient residency online, 2022
